rows.notes: pass-oi-default-scalar now emits three deterministic seed-indexed trigger-smoke cases for direct, local-carried, and local.tee/commuted OI-D shapes instead of one fixed module. The count-3 grouped OI-D sweep reaches comparison and reports three raw mismatches with no validation/generator/property/command failures; classify them as open scalar parity evidence until implemented or measured, not automatic semantic-safe drift. Future work should replace the seed-indexed smoke selector with randomized trigger-biased scalar constructors and wrappers around existing producers. The sweep runner prints result summaries (and can load them with --summarize-existing), so future OI-D/OI-E triage should start from profile-case and case-label status groups before opening individual WAT diffs. A 2026-06-27 representative diff pass classified the three trigger labels in classifiedCaseLabels. The narrow literal double-eqz final-folding candidate is now implemented and covered. The fresh count-3 OI-D sweep shows residual mismatches only from Starshine extra folds of constant compare and/or constant sign-extension shapes; those sampled residuals are measured smaller Starshine outputs (-5/-2/-2 canonical wasm bytes) and all outputs validate, but the row remains open until broader randomized trigger coverage and/or source-backed family policy decides whether to retain or align these output-shape wins.

classifiedCaseLabels.agentClassification: measured size-winning Starshine output drift over pure constant compare and constant sign-extension folds

rows.notes: oi-if-select-shell, oi-effectful-sibling, oi-trapping-sibling, and oi-commuted-operands are implemented-smoke and forwarded by the sweep runner. The first grouped count-4 OI-F sweep classifies all four transform labels before one-off probes: pure if/select, dead trapping sibling, and commuted-constant representatives are sampled canonical-size Starshine-win candidates under their transform contracts. Follow-up red-first slices reduced oi-effectful-sibling by sinking same-global if arms to global.set(if(result ...)) in HOT and raw-skip paths, folding sampled dropped/nested integer constants, rewriting sampled raw-skipped nonnegative signed compare/div/rem/shift spellings to unsigned forms, and matching the sampled finite float sub/div spellings. rows.binaryenOracleMode: compare-pass normalized WAT with all-features; focused rows must keep trap-relaxed zero-size mode separate
rows.blockerBoundaryReason: The OI-G trigger profile has nine seed-indexed/BodyStream-randomized trigger-smoke-plus cases: direct tiny i32 bulk/load-store, memory64 dynamic/local-carried bulk, live private-global/dead-trap, existing-producer/no-param-call wrapped bulk, narrow-store masks, dynamic i32 local.tee bulk operands, multi-memory bulk operations, seed-derived random-address copy/read, and seed-derived non-primary-memory random-address copy/read. The metamorphic layer now includes live zero/nonzero copy/fill/read, non-primary-memory self-copy/read, private-destination cross-memory copy/read, varied/dynamic/end-byte copy/read, and fixed/dynamic no-op atomic load/store/RMW/cmpxchg smoke. Prior canonical size-losing store-mask, live-effect/commuted, signed div/rem-by-zero, end-fill memory.size*65536, dynamic self-copy, and constant memory-offset residuals were fixed red-first rather than classified away. Latest grouped evidence .tmp/oi-g-cross-copy-boundary-count216b-20260629 compares 216/216 with 181 normalized matches, 35 mismatches, zero validation/generator/property/command failures, Binaryen cache hits/misses 173/43, zero canonical size-losing failure dirs, and five raw-size-larger Starshine dirs. The new private-destination cross-memory transform samples 12 cases with 11 matches; its one residual is the known store-mask low-byte-mask candidate, and representative WAT shows both tools lower the cross-memory copy to load8/store8. OI-G remains open on grouped mismatch reduction, xchg, wait/notify, randomized/multi-memory atomic addresses beyond fixed/dynamic smoke, true live memory effect/trap ordering, broader address/store commutation, randomized memory64/multi-memory/cross-memory mutation wrappers, raw-size residuals, and broader generator evidence. OI-J descriptor/exactness/TNH/IIT is unrelated and remains blocked pending true descriptor-compatible evidence.

classifiedCaseLabels.label: transform:oi-memory-size-boundary
classifiedCaseLabels.agentClassification: sampled canonical-size Starshine-win candidate after OI-G tiny bulk-memory lowering parity for the dead-shell smoke contract, with raw-size residual
classifiedCaseLabels.observedDiff: The transform prepends dead memory.copy/memory.fill variants for sizes 0/1/2/3/4/5/8/16. After fix4, Binaryen and Starshine both lower sampled size 1/2/4/8/16 variants and both leave 10 memory.copy plus 10 memory.fill operations; v128.load/store counts are 2/4 on both sides. The WAT diff is dominated by Starshine folding constant ordinary/atomic memory addresses into immediates where Binaryen keeps nonzero i32.const addresses (186 offset-bearing memory ops vs Binaryen 104) plus one fewer function-0 i32 local. Section dumps localize all measured size deltas to code: raw code Starshine/Binaryen 6162/6100 bytes, canonical code 6080/6108. Raw wasm is Starshine/Binaryen 6291/6229 bytes, canonical wasm is 6209/6237 bytes, and all raw/canonical outputs validate. A later count-110 after raw constant memory access offset folding samples memory-size-boundary ten times with eight matches and two remaining store-mask raw-size residual mismatches; the old direct-tiny/memory64 offset-folding residuals are gone.
classifiedCaseLabels.nextAction: Keep as a sampled candidate only for the synthetic dead-shell contract. Reopen as an OI-G parity gap if memory opcode counts drift again, if live effect/trap ordering or dynamic/memory64 operands fail, if canonical size regresses, or if a raw-byte signoff lane decides to align the extra offset-folding/local-section structure.

rows.notes: oi-call-ref-target-wrapper is implemented-smoke and forwarded by the sweep runner: it appends a private no-op target and prepends direct ref.func/call_ref, block-returned ref.func/call_ref, typed-select target/call_ref, and dead call_ref shells to existing defined functions. A default GenValid one-case compare produced one raw mismatch with no validation/generator/command failures; the earlier pass-oi-call-ref seed-0x5eed validity blocker from stringref/exn ref pools is fixed and the profile-backed smoke now reaches comparison. The profile-backed representative first exposed a large size-losing OI-H parity gap where Binaryen directized synthetic ref.func call_ref wrappers while Starshine left call_ref/ref.func spelling. The raw skip path now directizes adjacent ref.func/block-ref.func, constant-select ref.func, and single-use local.set(ref.func)/local.get call_ref/return_call_ref wrappers, and it trims guaranteed null-as-non-null, dropped-unreachable-block, dropped null ref.test/ref.cast, and dropped non-null ref.cast trap tails before returning a skipped function, without widening the full HOT raw gate. The latest one-case target-wrapper smoke still mismatches, but OI-H call target spelling is no longer the sampled residual: Starshine and Binaryen both have 0 call_ref/table.get residuals, 20 direct `call $5` occurrences, 0 ref.test/ref.cast/br_on_cast/br_on_cast_fail, and five `unreachable` roots. The remaining 18-byte raw size gap is local-section/ref.func-drop output shape drift: Starshine drops the inserted `ref.func` uses while Binaryen keeps five dropped `ref.func` instructions. A follow-up count-3 grouped OI-H sweep at `.tmp/oi-h-target-wrapper-grouped-sweep-20260627` mixed the target-wrapper, local-carried, and effectful-sibling transforms; all three cases still mismatched, but every sampled Starshine and Binaryen output had call_ref=0, return_call_ref=0, and table.get=0. The target-wrapper case remains the local-section/ref.func-drop shape drift; the local-carried case is local-section/dropped-null shape drift after successful call_ref cleanup; the effectful-sibling case is cross-family effectful private-global if/scalar spelling drift after successful call_ref cleanup. Future OI-H work should run broader table/local/return_call_ref/multivalue sweeps to find true remaining call_ref directization gaps instead of over-mining these cross-family residuals.
